Nazi Looted Booksat Penn Libraries
Markings guideCasesRegisterSourcesAbout
/about/gaps/

What we cannot account for

Most institutional registers stop at what they hold. This one keeps a page for what it does not. The numbers below are not failures to hide in a footnote; they are part of the record.

2,391–5,549
Believed sent, per Offenbach shipping records

Two surviving tallies disagree. We hold ~2,100. The difference — somewhere between three hundred and three-and-a-half thousand volumes — was dispersed, destroyed, or is shelved unrecognised.

~250–300
“Maybe” cases not yet placed

Books with a faint mark, an ambiguous inscription, or a catalog note but no surviving stamp. Held in a working list, not yet in the register.

unknown
Shelves never surveyed

The identification came from shelf-reading. Whole classes of the collection have not yet been read against the databases. We cannot state how many looted books remain unread — only that some do.

This is a page you can cite. If you are searching for a specific book and do not find it here, its absence may mean it was never sent, or it may mean we have not found it yet. We would rather you know which.

Penn Libraries · Provenance of looted books · prototypeUncertainty in this record is shown, not hidden.
